title: COVID-crash stock watchlist (March 22, 2020)
html:
TL;DR

  • 11 days into the COVID market crash, 17-year-old Yahir is building a four-stock watchlist: Carnival Corporation (cruise — "down, steady"), Boeing ("too important to lose"), Dave & Buster's (incomplete thesis), Beyond Meat ("long-term innovative").
  • Three "people" referenced as research/contact targets: Travis Boersma (Dutch Bros founder, Grants Pass local-hero entrepreneur), Nick (Computers — likely a small-business owner), Noah Martinez.
  • Two-tier reasoning shows: macro ("too important to lose" = government-backstop thesis on Boeing) + secular ("long-term innovative" = disruption thesis on Beyond Meat) — both crude versions of frames Yahir would refine over the next four years into the Austin thesis.
  • Companion to the exogenous event note two days later and the broader pandemic watchlist eight days later — late March 2020 is the densest stretch of pandemic-investing notes in Yahir's vault.
  • Marks the start of Yahir treating local Grants Pass adults (Boersma, Nick) as research nodes rather than just neighbors — the mentors network forming in real time.

Note

A 17-line note: four tickers, three names, no prices. The selection logic is visible — cruise (max-COVID-pain), aerospace (too-big-to-fail), restaurants (recovery play), alt-protein (secular). Yahir at the absolute peak of the crash, not fleeing risk but building a watchlist. The Boersma line is significant: Dutch Bros HQ is in Grants Pass, so Boersma is a visible local whom Yahir is treating as a research starting point — the hometown mentor instinct applied to investing. Nick "Computers" reads as another GP local whose business name doubled as his identifier.

Connections

  • carnival corp · boeing · beyond meat · travis boersma
  • 2020 03 24 exogenous event 911 — macro thesis companion
  • 2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist — broader watchlist 8 days later
  • grants pass teen era · covid pandemic era
